# Dependency Exclusions

#### ! Scenario



This scenario demonstrates exclusions.

#### ! Example

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 4.4
graph LR
    Root[Project Root] --> LibA[lib-a:1.0.0]
    LibA -.-> LibC[lib-c:1.0.0]
    Root -- exclude --> LibC
```

#### ! Example

```{.scala include="build.sbt" snippet="s5-deps"}
```

#### ! Expect
`lib-c` is pruned from the transitive graph under the declaring dependency.

#### ! Why
SBT (via Ivy/Coursier) uses the `.exclude(group, artifact)` method to prune the specified artifact from the transitive graph. This mechanism is why the same graph behaves differently across tools.

#### ! Fix
The most common pattern is swapping logging frameworks: excluding log4j and adding Logback. Some teams also exclude outdated or vulnerable transitives and replace them with newer versions declared directly.

#### ! Risk
* LOW Replacing an implementation (e.g., `log4j` with `logback`).
* MEDIUM Excluding a transitive thought to be unused.
* HIGH Excluding to "fix" conflicts without technical verification; this risks `NoClassDefFoundError` at runtime.

#### ! Check
**Fast check:** `sbt dependencyTree`

**What to look for:** Absence of the dependency under the declaring artifact.

**Conclusion:** The exclusion successfully prunes the dependency from this path.

#### ! Scales
In large Scala projects, exclusions accumulate as different library ecosystems bring in conflicting versions of common dependencies (particularly around Scala version suffixes and cross-builds). Exclusion management is one of the primary maintenance burdens in SBT builds.

#### ! Takeaway
Exclusions work.

#### ! Related
N/A

#### ! Wild

A recurring issue in Scala ecosystems occurs when libraries depend on different [Scala binary versions](https://docs.scala-lang.org/overviews/core/binary-compatibility-of-scala-releases.html) (for example, 2.12 vs 2.13). Because Scala encodes the binary version into artifact names (e.g., `library_2.12` vs `library_2.13`), mixing versions causes `NoSuchMethodError` or `ClassNotFoundException` at runtime. Teams often resolve this by excluding the unwanted variant and adding a direct dependency on the aligned version. The [sbt cross-building documentation](https://www.scala-sbt.org/1.x/docs/Cross-Build.html) covers the underlying mechanism.
